title: ranunculus hederaceus subsp. omiophyllus
intro: Ranunculus hederaceus subsp. omiophyllus, also known as the hairy buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus hederaceus plant, a perennial herb that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus hederaceus subsp. omiophyllus include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
